channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A heart-shaped note was found in socks bound for Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Someone tucked a heart-shaped note of encouragement into socks packed for Luigi Mangione to wear to court last month in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case, according to court documents made public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KTLA 5

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish national who is a doctoral student at Tufts University has been detained by federal agents without explanation, her lawyer said Wednesday. Rumeysa Ozturk, 30, had just left her home in Somerville to meet with friends Tuesday night when she was detained by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: NATO clarifies comments that four missing US soldiers had died during training in Lithuania
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — NATO on Wednesday clarified comments that Secretary-General Mark Rutte made earlier in the day, when he suggested that four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania had died, even though the U.S. Army said their fate was not yet confirmed.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The White House says nothing classified was shared on Signal. Democrats say that strains credulity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration struggled Wednesday to stem the fallout from revelations that top national security officials discussed sensitive attack plans over a messaging app and mistakenly added a journalist to the chain.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, STEPHEN GROVES, and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Inflatable baby protest highlights child care budget gap
item.description: Activists and lawmakers rallied outside Gov. Kathy Hochul’s Albany office with a 15-foot inflatable baby to protest severe gaps in child care funding.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order from U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts to slash the federal bureaucracy. A three-judge panel on the U.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: The White House security breach raises concerns among some military spouses and veterans
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — When Alyssa Myatt’s husband served on an aircraft carrier last year, she and other U.S. Navy spouses had to follow strict security protocols that meant driving to the ship’s home port just to learn that its deployment was being extended.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and ALLEN G. BREED, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Teens arrested after tractor-trailers struck by objects dropped from overpass
item.description: Two juvenile males, aged 16 and 17, have been charged with reckless endangerment after they reportedly threw multiple objects from an I-640 overpass.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, NewsNation

item.title: Congressional Republicans target PBS, NPR funding in contentious hearing
item.description: A House Republican pushing the Trump administration's government efficiency efforts has called for dismantling and defunding the nation's public broadcast system following a contentious public hearing on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NTSB chairwoman says reviewing the data after midair crash may prevent the next aviation accident
item.description: Someone should have spotted the alarming number of near misses in the skies over the nation's capital before the fatal midair collision that killed 67 people in January, and reviewing the data now could prevent future crashes, according to the head of the agency investigating the crash.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: NATO issues new statement on fate of 4 missing Fort Stewart soldiers
item.description: WIESBADEN, Germany (WSAV) -- The NATO Acting spokesperson said that the Secretary General was referring to news articles and did not confirm the death of four U.S. Army 3rd Infantry Division soldiers reported missing in Lithuania Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked agents handcuffing her
item.description: Rumeysa Ozturk's whereabouts are unknown and no known charges have been filed against her, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Babysitter checking for monsters finds man hiding under child's bed
item.description: A 27-year-old man has been arrested after a Kansas babysitter found him hiding under a child's bed, police said. At around 10:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KTLA 5

item.title: Street dog rescue saves more than 1,000 Texas animals
item.description: "I literally cry with every single dog, because I watch these dogs that have been so abused and so neglected, and they learn to be part of a family," Sasha Aghili said.
item.dc:creator: Avery Travis, NewsNation

item.title: Kermit the Frog to give commencement address at University of Maryland
item.description: Prominent political figures and lawmakers on the commencement speaker circuit are about to have some friendly, froggy competition.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-NFL player charged after 190 dogs seized from Oklahoma dog fighting venture
item.description: An Oklahoma man is facing federal charges after officials seized 190 dogs from an animal fighting venture, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Loved ones search for missing 21-year-old Southern California man
item.description: Emmanuel Boamah, 21, is described as a man standing 5 feet 10 inches tall and weighing around 150 pounds.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Wednesday asked the Supreme Court to allow it to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats take hope from upset win in a GOP-leaning Pennsylvania state Senate district
item.description: Democrat James Andrew Malone has narrowly won a special election for a Pennsylvania state Senate seat in a stretch of Republican-leaning suburbs and farming communities.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo And Marc Levy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration goes to Supreme Court to block teacher preparation grants
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court Wednesday to allow it to block federal teacher preparation grants that lower courts have ordered resumed.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Maryland county sheriff renews efforts to solve serial rape cold case
item.description: Charles County Sheriff's Office detectives in Maryland are asking for the public's help to identify a man accused of raping multiple women from 1996-2003.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Democrats demand answers from Trump team about war plans Signal chat
item.description: The top Democrats on the House’s Defense, Oversight, Foreign Affairs, and Intelligence committees want answers from President Trump’s national security team, saying they’ve become “deeply troubled” after a journalist was unknowingly added to a group chat of senior officials discussing war plans.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: The Boston skating community hosts the world championships even as it mourns plane crash victims
item.description: NORWOOD, Mass. (AP) — The chairs where Jinna Han and Spencer Lane used to sit are still there, and covered with gifts: cards and cookies, plants and pictures — anything that might have sparked a connection for their friends and fellow skaters.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles district attorney wants to bring back death penalty
item.description: Los Angeles District Attorney Nathan Hochman's office announced Tuesday that under limited circumstances, prosecutors could resume seeking the death penalty.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois returns stolen land to Prairie Band Potawatomi Nation
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The Prairie Land Potawatomi Nation has reclaimed land in Illinois that was promised to the tribe's leader 175 years ago but stolen by the federal government 20 years later. A law signed by Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order targeting law firm that employed Mueller team prosecutor
item.description: President Trump signed an order Tuesday targeting a law firm that previously employed Andrew Weissmann, a former federal prosecutor who worked on then-special counsel Robert Mueller’s team.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: United Airlines flight to China diverted to San Francisco after pilot forgets passport
item.description: A United Airlines flight headed to Shanghai, China, had to turn around over the weekend after the pilot forgot to bring a passport, the airline company said.
item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chick-fil-A unveils new item you can't find in restaurants
item.description: Chick-fil-A is rolling out a new bottled dressing that fans won’t find at any of its 3,100 U.S. restaurants.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Nevada investigates more than 300 potential voter fraud cases from 2024 election
item.description: After the 2024 election, the state of Nevada says it is investigating more than 300 cases of people attempting to vote twice in the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York girls basketball coach fired for hair-pulling incident with player apologizes
item.description: The Northville Central School District announced the firing of highs school girls basketball head coach Jim Zullo following a postgame incident with one of the Northville players. The incident occurred following Northville's loss in the Class D state championship game.
item.dc:creator: Griffin Haas, ABC 4

item.title: Police: Third person arrested in New Mexico shooting that killed 3, injured 15
item.description: A second teenager has been arrested following a deadly shooting at a New Mexico park, marking the third person taken into custody as part of the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, ABC 4

item.title: New Hampshire settles youth center abuse case for $10 million
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — The New Hampshire attorney general’s office has agreed to a $10 million settlement in the case of a man who alleged that he was gang-raped in a stairwell at the state’s youth detention center in the 1990s.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4
